claim: Recurrent mutations occur frequently within CTCF anchor sites adjacent to oncogenes.

result.claim: Recurrent mutations occur frequently within CTCF anchor sites adjacent to oncogenes.
result.reasoning: The claim that "Recurrent mutations occur frequently within CTCF anchor sites" is supported by evidence from paper 5373138, which states that "CTCF anchor regions are a frequent site of mutations in cancer cells" (prop_1813). The abstract of this paper further emphasizes that these regions are "a frequent site of mutations in cancer cells, underscoring their functional importance in cellular regulation." However, the evidence does not explicitly confirm the "adjacent to oncogenes" part of the claim. While the paper discusses the regulatory landscape and gene expression, it does not directly link the recurrent mutations in CTCF anchor sites to their proximity to oncogenes. Therefore, while the first part of the claim is supported, the full claim has some limitations in the provided evidence.

evidence.text: Background Checkpoint kinase 2 (CHEK2) averts cancer development by promoting cell cycle arrest and activating DNA repair in genetically damaged cells.
evidence.source: Linkage Disequilibrium Mapping of       CHEK2: Common Variation and Breast Cancer Risk

evidence.text: Cells expressing high amounts of CSF1R (CSF1Rhigh cells) showed potent leukemia-initiating activity.
evidence.source: PU.1-mediated upregulation of CSF1R is crucial for leukemia stem cell potential induced by MOZ-TIF2

evidence.text: TLX1 and TLX3 transcription factor oncogenes have a key role in the pathogenesis of T cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia (T-ALL).
evidence.source: Reverse engineering of TLX oncogenic transcriptional networks identifies RUNX1 as tumor suppressor in T-ALL

evidence.text: Mutations in the NT5C2 gene were found in 1/35 (3%) relapse B-precursor ALLs.
evidence.source: Activating mutations in the NT5C2 nucleotidase gene drive chemotherapy resistance in relapsed ALL

evidence.text: Rare causal mutations create genome-wide significant synthetic associations.
evidence.source: Rare Variants Create Synthetic Genome-Wide Associations

evidence.text: A retrospective statistical analysis of the Centre’s database identified an N348I mutation in the RT connection domain in treatment-experienced individuals.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: 18 mutations were found in 6,912 individuals with breast cancer.

evidence.text: CTCF anchor regions are conserved across species.

evidence.text: Each strand of the CoYMV genome is interrupted by site-specific discontinuities.
